Finding.Description: Low Level Requirements document LL-10.xml content was not provided. Without LL-10 content, we cannot verify refinements, dependencies, or traceability, including interactions with LL-LOC-7.1.
Finding.Solution: Provide LL-10.xml contents (or paste its LL-10.x requirements) so I can verify refinements to HL requirements, LL-LOC-7.1 interactions, and traceability. After receipt, I will generate a full Findings, Justifications, and Implementations document and perform complete traceability checks.

Finding.Description: High-Level Requirement HL-MAP-2.2 uses the misspelled name "Cell Clasification". This spelling discrepancy affects traceability, reviews, and tooling consistency across HL and LL documents.
Finding.Solution: Rename HL-MAP-2.2 to "Cell Classification" and propagate the corrected name to all references and tooling. Ensure LL-MAP-2.2 aligns with the corrected HL name and update any dependent LL references accordingly.

Finding.Description: LL-LOC-7.1 currently shows potential redundancy by having a DependsOn LL-EXPL-1.1 alongside a Refines HL-EXPL-1.1. If this redundancy exists, it can impair traceability clarity and risk cycles.
Finding.Solution: Ensure LL-LOC-7.1 uses a single trace mechanism. Suggested Relationship set: Refines HL-EXPL-1.1; DependsOn LL-EXPL-1.2; DependsOn LL-INTERF-6.1; DependsOn LL-MAP-2.1. Remove any redundant DependsOn LL-EXPL-1.1 to maintain an acyclic, unambiguous traceability graph.

Finding.Description: Consistency between HL and LL naming for Cell Classification should be maintained. HL currently uses a misspelled name, which can propagate inconsistencies into LL mappings and traceability tooling.
Finding.Solution: Align HL naming with LL by renaming HL-MAP-2.2 to "Cell Classification" and propagate this corrected term to LL references (e.g., LL-MAP-2.2). Validate traceability mappings from HL to LL after the change.
